name: Reinforcement learning
definition: Agents learn action-value or policy estimates from reward feedback to maximize cumulative reward.

name: Prospect theory
definition: Decisions under risk weight gains and losses asymmetrically against a reference point, with diminishing sensitivity.

name: Bounded rationality
definition: Agents use heuristics under cognitive limits, satisficing rather than optimizing.

name: Free-energy principle
definition: Agents minimize the divergence between predicted and observed sensory states by acting and updating beliefs.

name: Expected utility theory
definition: Decisions maximize expected utility computed from outcome probabilities and a utility function.

name: Drift-diffusion model
definition: Choice and reaction time arise from noisy accumulation of evidence to a decision threshold.

name: Bayesian brain hypothesis
definition: Perception and decision-making are posterior inference combining priors with likelihoods.

name: Homeostatic regulation
definition: Behavior restores internal variables to set points via error-driven control.

name: Active inference
definition: Agents act to minimize expected free energy, integrating perception and policy selection under generative models.

name: Optimal foraging theory
definition: Agents allocate time/effort across patches to maximize long-run intake rate, e.g. via the Marginal Value Theorem.
